releaseDate.title: Road to Vostok Release Date
releaseDate.intro: Road to Vostok launched into Steam Early Access on April 7, 2026. The current launch version is Build 1, called Road, and it ships with the core survival loop, expanded maps, dynamic systems, and multi-shelter support.

items.label: Day-one feature pillars
items.value: Gameplay loop, expanded maps, dynamic elements, and multi-shelter support

items.label: Launch content highlights
items.value: Event system, world map, crafting menu, two new game modes, four new maps, four new shelters, two new traders, and a long launch changelog

beginnerGuide.title: Road to Vostok Beginner Guide
beginnerGuide.intro: Your first goal is not to force a border push. Start in Area 05, get comfortable with looting, healing, traders, and shelter routines, then move east only after your economy and gear feel stable.

steps.title: Begin in Area 05
steps.description: Area 05 is the starting region and the foundation of the game loop. This is where you find your first shelters, traders, starter loot, and early tasks before you even think about crossing east.

steps.title: Secure a shelter and treat it as your base
steps.description: The early loop is built around your shelter. Use it to stabilize your run, manage what you keep, and avoid playing every outing like an all-in gamble.

steps.title: Learn the basic survival loop first
steps.description: Focus on simple looting, healing, and extraction habits before chasing danger. Early practice should be about staying alive, understanding item value, and keeping your gear loss low.

steps.title: Get comfortable with weapons and ammo handling
steps.description: Road to Vostok expects you to understand loading, ammo management, and weapon readiness. Learn that routine early so combat mistakes do not come from your own inventory handling.

steps.title: Use traders and tasks to build momentum
steps.description: Trading and tasks are core progression tools, not side content. They help you turn early scavenging into a steadier economy and prepare you for harder runs.

steps.title: Do not rush the Border Zone
steps.description: Border crossings are dangerous by design. Different crossing points can involve mines, physical obstacles, or waterways, and the area is guarded by armed forces with air support.

steps.title: Treat Vostok like a high-stakes endgame push
steps.description: Dying in Area 05 or the Border Zone costs what you carry, but dying in Vostok wipes everything. Only push there when your route, gear, and recovery plan are ready.

quickTips: Area 05 has shelters, traders, and tasks - use all three before pushing east.
Your shelter is your anchor. Lose it and your stability collapses.
Ammo and weapon readiness matter more here than in most shooters.
Tasks are not side content - they are your economy and progression engine.
The Border Zone is guarded. Plan your crossing, not just your gear.

maps.title: Road to Vostok Maps
maps.intro: The official game structure starts in Area 05, pushes through the Border Zone, and ends in Vostok. Build 1 adds named launch maps inside that structure.

items.title: Area 05
items.summary: Area 05 is the evacuated zone in southeastern Finland where your run begins. It contains shelters, traders, tasks, and the starting loot path for early survival.

items.title: School
items.summary: School is one of the named Build 1 launch maps in Area 05. It belongs to the early-game side of the route and fits the part of the loop where players build stability.

items.title: Highway
items.summary: Highway is another Build 1 Area 05 map. It expands the early-game route options and gives players more room to learn movement, looting, and risk management.

items.title: Outpost
items.summary: Outpost is the third named Build 1 map in Area 05. Players can combine shelter use, trader access, and early task progression here.

items.title: Border Zone
items.summary: Each Border Zone map has a crossing point with different challenges. Some crossings involve mines or physical obstacles, others require waterways and boats, and the whole line is guarded.

items.title: Vostok
items.summary: Vostok is the high-risk, high-reward side located in Russia. Every Vostok map is a permadeath map, making route planning and exit discipline far more important than in Area 05.

items.title: Terminal
items.summary: Terminal is the named Build 1 Vostok map. It sits on the permadeath side of the game, so it works best as a later push once players already understand the Area 05 economy.

roadmap.title: Road to Vostok Roadmap
roadmap.intro: The official roadmap lists eight named builds. Build 1 is already live, Build 2 and Build 3 are dated for 2026, and the later phases are announced with feature themes but without public release dates.

items.heading: Build 1 - Road
items.summary: The Early Access launch build with core survival loop and expanded content.

items.heading: Build 2 - Nomads
items.summary: The first post-launch roadmap milestone focused on living factions and systemic encounters.

items.heading: Build 3 - Signal
items.summary: The next major milestone introduces more directed progression and world flavor.

items.heading: Build 5 - North
items.summary: A systems-heavy update built around seasons, travel, and wildlife.

items.heading: Build 6 - Abyss
items.summary: A maritime expansion of the game world.

items.heading: Build 8 - Silent Night
items.summary: The final roadmap phase aimed at a more complete campaign layer.

systemRequirements.title: Can Your PC Run Road to Vostok?
systemRequirements.subtitle: Official minimum and recommended requirements for the current Early Access build, with a quick checklist so you can answer in seconds.
systemRequirements.intro: The Steam page lists two hardware tiers, both targeting a dedicated GPU setup with 64-bit Windows 10 and DirectX 12. Minimum gets you into the game; recommended is the safer target for the developer's higher-end baseline.
quickCheck: 64-bit Windows 10 is the only OS listed
8 GB RAM required on both tiers
DirectX 12 is required on both tiers
Steam notes requirements may change in Early Access

weaponsGuide.title: Road to Vostok Weapons, Ammo, and Attachments
weaponsGuide.subtitle: A practical overview of weapon families, named calibers, and attachment lanes in the current build.
weaponsGuide.intro: Road to Vostok has a broad spread of firearm types, from lightweight pistols to precision rifles. Separate ammunition families, optic lanes, and suppressor compatibility mean gear choice matters before you even leave the shelter.

items.keyFact: 3 pistols listed — lightweight primary or secondary carry option

items.examples: AK-12, AKM, AKS-74U, RK-95, MK18, VSS
items.keyFact: Broadest general-purpose family, 600–800 RPM range across calibers
Mosin 50 dmg at 10 RPM; SVD 60 dmg at 200 RPM — range identity over fire rate
One-shot up to 2 m, two-shot up to 5 m — strongest close-range panic weapon

items.examples: MRM, SRO, PRO, EXPS, Kobra, ACOG, PU, Hybrid
items.keyFact: Short / medium / long range groups plus suppressor options
Ammo stacks range from 100 to 300 per caliber — match gun to caliber before loading out

zoneGuide.title: Road to Vostok Zone Guide: Area 05, Border Zone, and Vostok
zoneGuide.subtitle: The three-part progression path from your starting zone to the permadeath endgame, with clear risk thresholds for each crossing.
zoneGuide.intro: Road to Vostok is built around moving east through increasingly dangerous territory. Area 05 is your setup space, the Border Zone is the crossing challenge, and Vostok is the all-in end of the route where loot spikes and punishment becomes absolute.

items.heading: Area 05
items.summary: The starting zone in southeastern Finland
details: Your road begins in Area 05, an evacuated zone in southeastern Finland.
Contains shelters, traders, tasks, and starting loot for initial survival.
Bandits are the main hostile force disrupting the area.
Death here costs only the equipment and loot you are carrying.

items.heading: Border Zone
items.summary: The guarded crossing between Finland and Russia
details: Once geared up, your route continues east into the Border Zone.
Each Border Zone map has a crossing point with different challenges.
Crossings may involve mines, physical obstacles, or waterways with boats.
Guards control the border and may call in air support to stop crossings.

items.heading: Vostok
details: If you manage to cross the Border Zone, you enter Vostok in Russia.
Vostok holds the most valuable loot in the game.
Every Vostok map is a permadeath map — death loses everything.
The Military controls the zone with heavy weaponry and armored vehicles.

items.heading: When to Attempt the Crossing
items.summary: Go east only after your run is stable
details: The official flow is Area 05 first, then Border Zone, then Vostok.
Trigger for moving east: gather enough loot and equipment in Area 05.
Area 05 and Border Zone deaths cost your gear, but Vostok is all-or-nothing.
Push for Vostok only after your base setup and kit feel ready.

shelterCustomization.title: How Shelters Work in Road to Vostok
shelterCustomization.subtitle: Shelters are more than decoration — they are your save system, storage layer, and long-term home network.
shelterCustomization.intro: Shelters are one of the clearest identity features in Road to Vostok. They are where you save, store loot, shape your layout, unlock new homes through progression, and gradually build a safer life before taking bigger risks farther east.

items.title: Save and Store
items.description: Shelters are safe zones and the only way to save your game. They also hold your stored loot, making them central to every long run.

items.title: Multiple Homes
items.description: You often need multiple shelters when traveling east. Multi-shelter support was confirmed in Build 1, released on April 7, 2026.

items.title: Different Shelter Types
items.description: Shelters range from small cabins to large concrete bunkers. Some are unlocked only through keys earned from completing trader tasks.

items.title: Decoration Mode
items.description: Every shelter starts with a default furniture template, but decoration mode lets you catalog, move, and rotate furniture — new items earned via crafting or trading.

items.title: Free Item Placement
items.description: Place individual items wherever you want. Items are physics-based and their positions save automatically when you exit the shelter.

tradersGuide.title: Road to Vostok Traders Guide
tradersGuide.subtitle: Learn who the current traders are, what each one specializes in, and how the barter loop works in Early Access.
tradersGuide.intro: Road to Vostok uses a barter economy instead of a traditional cash shop. In Area 05, traders and their task chains are part of the main survival loop, so smart routing is not just about loot runs but also about knowing which trader solves your immediate problem.

items.title: Generalist
items.description: The Generalist is the broad-purpose trader near the Village shelter and is the most flexible stop for early progression.
bullets: Trades a wide mix of goods: food, water, crafting materials, quest items, medical supplies, and weapons.
Restocks every 10 minutes; leaving and re-entering Shelter can refresh random stock faster.
Best early stop when you need a little of everything instead of one specialist category.

items.title: Doctor
items.description: The Doctor is the dedicated medical trader on Shipyard and is the cleanest source for healing-oriented supplies.
bullets: Focuses on medical items such as bandages, medkits, and tourniquets.
Restocks every 10 minutes with randomized stock after each refresh.
Most useful when your run is limited by healing, status management, or med-related task turn-ins.

items.title: How Trading Works
items.description: The game is built around barter rather than a normal buy-and-sell currency loop.
bullets: There is no standard cash economy in the main player-facing trade loop; value comes from what you carry.
Generalist accepts almost any valuable item and turns mixed loot into practical survival gear.
Doctor is better used when you want to convert loot value into medicine and recovery tools.

items.title: Upcoming Traders
items.description: The official roadmap already names several upcoming traders beyond the current Area 05 pair.

tasksGuide.title: Road to Vostok Tasks Guide
tasksGuide.subtitle: Track the current task flow, the items that matter most, and the progression blockers worth stashing early.
tasksGuide.intro: Early Access already includes trading and task progression in Area 05. The current task loop revolves around active trader hubs, key hand-in items, and utility pieces that can quietly block your progress if you sell them too fast.

rows.data: Current active task providers are Generalist and Doctor.
rows.why: These two traders anchor the present task loop in Area 05.
rows.keep: Check both trader pages and hand-ins before liquidating loot.

rows.data: Inverter and B Cables are listed as core task and power items.
rows.why: They are tied to shelter power and broader progression routing.
rows.keep: Keep every Inverter and B Cables item until your chain is complete.

rows.data: Polaris and Doctor's Notes appear in trader-task references.
rows.why: These are easy to mis-sell because they do not look like combat gear.
rows.keep: Stash Polaris and Doctor's Notes instead of dumping them in a barter cleanup.

rows.data: Task keys and trader keys are part of the known core-task item set.
rows.why: Keys can gate doors, routes, and task completion flow.
rows.keep: Hold every key until you know exactly which door or hand-in it supports.

rows.data: Doctor task examples include Cold Medicine, Duct Tape, Painkillers, Antibiotics, and Polaris.
rows.why: A lot of medical progression competes directly with your own survival usage.
rows.keep: Keep a reserve stack of meds and utility items instead of consuming everything.

rows.data: Generalist tasks include Makarov, AKM, RK-95, AK-12, batteries, books, and clothing.
rows.why: Several progression jumps depend on stash discipline rather than pure firefight skill.
rows.keep: Do not instantly sell duplicate early weapons, batteries, books, or unusual apparel.

multiplayer.title: Road to Vostok Multiplayer
multiplayer.subtitle: A clear answer to the co-op question: what exists now, why the game is single-player, and what might come later.
multiplayer.intro: Road to Vostok gets compared to extraction shooters all the time, so multiplayer is one of the first questions new players ask. The official answer is straightforward: the game is built as a single-player survival FPS first.

items.question: Does Road to Vostok have multiplayer right now?
items.answer: No. The Steam store lists Road to Vostok as a Single-player feature, and the official site describes it as a hardcore single-player survival game.

items.question: Is there co-op at launch or in Early Access?
items.answer: No launch co-op is listed on the official store page or FAQ. Early Access is centered on the solo survival loop: traders, tasks, map-to-map travel, hostile AI factions, and the permadeath Vostok zone.

items.question: Why was Road to Vostok designed without multiplayer first?
items.answer: The official FAQ says the project was designed from the beginning as a replayable single-player game because that gives the developer freedoms not practical in multiplayer production. Multiplayer projects in this genre are extremely expensive to build and maintain for a solo developer.

items.question: Could co-op or multiplayer be added to Road to Vostok later?
items.answer: Possibly, but only after a successful and feature-rich single-player version is complete. The official FAQ states co-op would only happen after that milestone is reached.

steamDeck.title: Road to Vostok Steam Deck
steamDeck.subtitle: See where Steam Deck support stands today, what Linux support looks like, and what control compromises portable players should expect.
steamDeck.intro: Road to Vostok is built as a PC-first survival FPS, so Steam Deck support is more about practical compatibility than a deck-native design target. The official platform plan and community play reports together give a useful picture of what portable players can expect.

items.question: Is Road to Vostok a Deck-first release?
items.answer: No. The official FAQ says the game is designed as a PC game focusing on Windows and Linux, with Windows as the main launch OS.

items.question: What is the Linux plan for Road to Vostok?
items.answer: The official FAQ says native Linux support is planned a few months after launch. That makes Linux support part of the roadmap, but not the primary launch target.

items.question: Can Road to Vostok run on Steam Deck today?
items.answer: Yes, playable reports already exist. ProtonDB lists the game with a Platinum Proton rating and shows Steam Deck Verified Status as Playable, and community posts include users reporting very strong performance on Deck hardware.

items.question: What about controller support in Road to Vostok?
items.answer: Full native controller support is not the main Early Access focus. Community discussion tied to the official FAQ states controller support is planned before the main release, and users already report the game can be played with controller setup work, but without aim assist.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Common questions about Road to Vostok answered

questions.question: Is Road to Vostok a singleplayer game?
questions.answer: Yes. Road to Vostok is a singleplayer-only PC survival FPS. There is no multiplayer or co-op mode, and the developers have not announced plans to add one.

questions.question: What platforms can I play Road to Vostok on?
questions.answer: Road to Vostok is available on Windows through Steam. The developers have mentioned Linux support as a future goal. Main console support is planned only after PC version 1.0.

questions.question: What happens if you die in Road to Vostok?
questions.answer: In Area 05 and the Border Zone, dying costs you what you are carrying. In Vostok, death is permanent and wipes your entire progress. This permadeath mechanic is the core risk that separates a normal run from a Vostok push.

questions.question: How much does Road to Vostok cost on Steam?
questions.answer: Road to Vostok launched at $14.99 during the Early Access introductory period, with a base price of $19.99 after the launch discount window ends.

questions.question: How long is the Road to Vostok Early Access period?
questions.answer: The developers estimate approximately 2 to 4 years of Early Access development. The official roadmap lists 8 named builds, and Build 1 launched on April 7, 2026.

questions.question: What is in the Road to Vostok Build 1 launch version?
questions.answer: Build 1 ships with the core gameplay loop, expanded maps including School, Highway, Outpost in Area 05 and Terminal in Vostok, dynamic elements, multi-shelter support, event system, world map, crafting menu, two new game modes, two new traders, and a large launch changelog of tasks, items, and systems.
